rulings.Case Title: BURT HOWARD ET AL VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 31, 2017, Tuesday, in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Duro Dyne Corporation's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Howard was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's separate statement demonstrated only that Mr. Howard has no personal knowledge whether any of the Duro Dyne flexible duct connectors with which he worked contained asbestos. That does not sustain defendant's burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs will be unable to prove their case by any means. Defendant's separate statement did not properly raise the sufficiency of plaintiffs' discovery responses on the topic of asbestos content. Even if it had, plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories would preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
